reasoning: Exchange rates exhibit near random-walk behavior over the medium-to-long term, making 0.5 an excellent base rate. The most recent available daily value (7.1785 on June 20) is situated at the lower end of the June mid-point range (approx 7.172 - 7.195), indicating a slightly higher than 50% chance of an upward mean reversion in the very short term. Major institutional forecasters (e.g., MUFG, UBS) predict the USD/CNY to drift upward structurally to the 7.20 - 7.30 range by the end of 2025 due to expected US tariff policies. Despite this, forecasting exchange rates more than a few months out is highly uncertain, as opposing macroeconomic forces (like PBOC intervention and Federal Reserve potential rate cuttings) offset one another. As instructed, certainty decreases over longer forecast horizons, returning exactly to baseline uncertainty (0.50) for the furthest date.
